quality.rationale: 概念辨析题，考查张量定义核心判据与坐标变换法则，需多步逻辑推导排除仿射叠加可能性，教学典型且结构可迁移

origin.question:
Problem 3.13. A two index "object" $\mathbf{X}^{\mu 
u}$ is defined by the "direct sum" of two vectors $\mathbf{X}^{\mu 
u} = \mathbf{A}^{\mu} + \mathbf{B}^{
u}$ . Is $\mathbf{X}^{\mu 
u}$ a tensor? Is there a transformation law to take $\mathbf{X}$ to a new coordinate system, i.e. to obtain $\mathbf{X}^{\hat{\mu}\hat{
u}}$ from $\mathbf{X}^{\mu 
u}$ ?
origin.answer:
It is not a tensor. Its coordinate transformation law is $\mathbf{X}^{\mu'
u'} = \Lambda_{a}^{\mu'}\mathbf{A}^{a} + \Lambda_{ eta}^{
u'}\mathbf{B}^{ eta}$. It cannot be expressed as a linear transformation $\mathbf{X}^{\mu'
u'} = \mathbf{T}^{\mu'
u'}_{\alpha eta}\mathbf{X}^{\alpha eta}$ due to the non-uniqueness of the decomposition $X=A+B$.
